task: Write a Python function range_summary(nums) taking a list of integers, possibly unsorted with duplicates, and returning a string of the distinct values in ascending order, collapsing consecutive runs into 'start-end' and joining pieces with commas, so [1, 2, 3, 5] becomes '1-3,5'. A single number stays a single number, never a range. An empty list returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function is mostly correct and handles the edge case of an empty list. The code could be more concise, but it is readable.
trace:
**
I solved this task by first handling the edge case of an empty input list. Then, I used a set to remove duplicates and sorted the numbers. I iterated over the sorted numbers to find consecutive runs, building ranges as I went along. When a range ended (either because the current number wasn't one more than the previous, or we reached the end of the list), I added it to the result list. Finally, I joined all the ranges together with commas and returned the string.
